location: Dropped by Lesser Kindred of Rot enemies. The easiest farm is the pair standing right outside Church of the Plague near Sellia, Caelid; a much larger cluster (roughly a dozen) inhabits the Grand Cloister at the south end of Lake of Rot, reached via Ranni's questline path beneath Liurnia.
tactics: The Church of the Plague pair is low-risk and quick to reset by resting at the nearby grace — best for a short farming session.
The Grand Cloister has far more Kindred of Rot per lap, but scarlet rot buildup from the surrounding Lake of Rot is a real hazard — bring rot resistance.
Drop rate is low, so expect to need multiple kills at either spot.
lore: A glaive fashioned from a hardened shell, carried by the rot-born pests that crawl out of the Swamp of Aeonia. Something in its make offers the wielder a slight edge against non-physical harm.

builds.name: Low-Investment Early Halberd
builds.why: Low Strength/Dexterity requirements (13/13) make it usable very early, and its passive slightly boosts non-physical damage negation while equipped.
